title: Herbal Solutions for Anxiety: 11 Tinctures to Calm Your Mind

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for anxiety because it helps to calm the mind and body by blocking the action of a chemical in the brain that can cause feelings of worry and fear.
Valeranone is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the body by interacting with the brain's neurotransmitters to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Valerenal is good for anxiety because it acts on the brain's GABA receptors, helping to calm the mind and reduce feelings of nervousness and stress.
Isovaleric acid may help reduce anxiety due to its ability to act as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that may help with anxiety. It contains a plant extract that affects the brain's chemistry, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. When taken, the tincture can slow down the brain's activity, making it easier to fall asleep and stay calm. This is because it contains a compound called valerenic acid, which works as a mild sedative. It also has a calming effect on the nervous system, making it a popular choice for people with anxiety. The tincture can be taken as needed, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects. In some cases, it may be more effective when taken with other stress-relieving herbs, such as passionflower or lavender.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures that help with anxiety, start by gathering high-quality green tea leaves. You will need 250 grams of the leaves for every 1 liter of tincture you plan to make. Combine the leaves with 70% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely submerged in the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the liquid extract the beneficial compounds from the leaves. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and take 20-30 drops of it as needed, ideally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for anxiety because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the brain, leading to a decrease in stress and anxiety levels by promoting relaxation and calmness.

Kaempferol is good for anxiety because it acts as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to calm the mind and body by reducing stress hormones and promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
Tryptophan is good for anxiety because it helps to increase the production of serotonin in the brain, a neurotransmitter that calms the mind and reduces feelings of stress and anxiety.

Flavonoids are good for anxiety because they have been shown to have a calming effect on the brain by reducing stress hormones like cortisol, promoting relaxation, and improving mood due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tincture is a natural remedy that helps with anxiety. The herb, also known as passionflower, has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. When you're feeling anxious, your brain is like a busy highway with too many cars. The chemicals in passionflower help slow down the traffic, making it easier to relax. It works by affecting the neurotransmitters in the brain, such as GABA and serotonin, which help regulate mood and calmness. This can lead to a sense of calm and reduced anxiety. The tincture can also help you fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, which can further reduce anxiety. Many people use passionflower tincture in combination with other natural remedies, like valerian root, to get the best results. Some people take it as needed, while others prefer to take it regularly to prevent anxiety from building up.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures that help with anxiety, you will need dried Camellia sinensis leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a dark place to store the tincture. First, fill the glass jar about 2/3 full with the dried Camellia sinensis leaves. Then, pour enough solvent over the leaves to cover them completely. Make sure the leaves are fully submerged and there's enough solvent to cover them. Close the jar and store it in a dark place, shaking the jar gently every day for 2-6 weeks. This process is called maceration, which helps to extract the active compounds from the Camellia sinensis leaves into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Camellia sinensis tincture is now ready to use, but make sure to store it in a cool, dark place. The recommended dosage is usually 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the mind and body by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which helps to ease symptoms of anxiety.

Linalyl acetate is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation by acting on the brain's GABA receptors, which helps to slow down rapid breathing and heart rate associated with anxiety.
Rosmarinic acid is good for anxiety because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and promote relaxation by blocking the production of stress hormones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, or English lavender, has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. The tincture of this herb is made by soaking the plant's flowers and leaves in a liquid, like ethanol or glycerin. When taken, the tincture releases chemicals that affect the brain's activity. It can slow down the heart rate and lower blood pressure, which can be beneficial for people who experience anxiety. The tincture also has a sedative effect, helping to quiet the mind and promote relaxation. This can be especially helpful for people who have trouble sleeping due to anxiety. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce anxiety-related physical symptoms like headaches and muscle tension. By reducing anxiety symptoms and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia tincture can help improve mood and overall well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures for anxiety relief, you'll need dried Camellia sinensis leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a high-proof alcohol like vodka or brandy. Start by measuring out one part of dried Camellia sinensis leaves to two parts of the alcohol in the glass jar. For example, if you're using one cup of dried leaves, you'll need two cups of vodka. Next, fill the jar with the leaves and pour the vodka over them, making sure that the leaves are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the leaves infuse into the alcohol. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Camellia sinensis tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts of a few drops under the tongue to help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal tinctures for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides, which is another name for green tea, help reduce anxiety by blocking the production of stress hormones and increasing the levels of neurotransmitters like serotonin that promote relaxation and calmness.

Saponins are good for anxiety because they have a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors to produce a soothing and tranquil feeling.
Avenacoside is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the mind and body, allowing individuals to feel more relaxed and less stressed.

Polyphenols are good for anxiety because they help reduce stress and anxiety by blocking the production of stress hormones like cortisol and promoting the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin that help calm the mind.
Flavonoids are good for anxiety because they have a calming effect on the body by increasing serotonin levels, which helps to reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oat extract, is a natural remedy used to help manage anxiety. This herb is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce feelings of stress and worry. Avena sativa tinctures contain compounds that may help slow down an overactive brain, allowing a person to feel more relaxed and centered. Some people find that taking Avena sativa tinctures helps them sleep better, which can also contribute to reduced anxiety levels. Additionally, Avena sativa may help reduce symptoms of anxiety by blocking the action of stress hormones in the body. When taken as directed, Avena sativa tinctures may be a safe and effective way to manage anxiety symptoms without the risk of side effects associated with prescription medications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures that help with anxiety, you will need dried tea leaves and a solvent such as vodka or glycerin. Start by measuring one part of dried Camellia sinensis leaves to three parts of your chosen solvent, then combine the two in a clean glass jar. Secure the lid tightly and let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the tea leaves release their properties into the solvent. After the infusion time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. If you're using vodka, you can now bottle the tincture and use it as is, but if you're using glycerin, you may need to add a little water to achieve the desired consistency. To use your tincture, take a few drops under your tongue or mix it with water, and start with small doses to adjust to its effects.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for anxiety because it acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant that helps to calm the nervous system and reduce stress by regulating the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin.

Bilobalide is good for anxiety because it has anti-anxiety properties that help reduce stress and promote relaxation by acting on the brain's GABA receptors and increasing the production of neurotransmitters that calm the mind.
Flavonoids, commonly found in green tea, are good for anxiety because they have a calming effect on the body by reducing the production of stress hormones and promoting relaxation.
Kaempferol is good for anxiety because it helps to calm the mind and body by reducing inflammation, stress hormones, and oxidative damage in the brain, promoting relaxation and a sense of well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is often used to help with anxiety because it contains chemicals that can calm the mind and body. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, which have been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years. Ginkgo biloba has a compound called flavonoids, which can help reduce stress and anxiety by regulating the body's response to stress. It also contains a compound called bilobalide, which can help improve sleep quality, which is often a problem for people with anxiety. When taken as a tincture, the flavonoids and bilobalide in Ginkgo biloba can be absorbed quickly into the bloodstream, where they can start to work to calm the mind and body. This can help reduce feelings of anxiety and worry, allowing people to feel more relaxed and in control.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Camellia sinensis tincture that may help with anxiety, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Camellia sinensis leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil such as glycerin or grapeseed oil, and a dark glass bottle for storage. Begin by measuring out one part of dried Camellia sinensis leaves to one part of carrier oil in the glass jar. For example, if using a 4-ounce jar, use 2 ounces of each ingredient. Then, fill the jar to the top with the dried leaves, covering them completely with the carrier oil. Secure the lid tightly and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken as needed, typically 20-30 drops in water or tea, and may help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Luteolin is good for anxiety because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and promote relaxation by blocking the production of stress hormones like cortisol in the brain.

Apigenin is good for anxiety because it helps to calm the nervous system by blocking the activity of certain stress chemicals, such as GABA receptors, which can lead to feelings of relaxation and reduced anxiety.

Rosmarinic acid is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the brain, reducing stress hormones and promoting relaxation by acting as an antioxidant and a natural inhibitor of inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tincture, also known as lemon balm tincture, is a natural remedy that can help with anxiety. The main active ingredient in lemon balm is rosmarinic acid, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. When taken, it helps to reduce feelings of stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Lemon balm tincture also contains antioxidants that help protect the body from free radicals, which can contribute to anxiety and other mental health issues. By promoting a good night's sleep and improving mood, lemon balm tincture can help reduce anxiety symptoms and improve overall well-being. Additionally, lemon balm tincture has been shown to have a positive effect on the brain's GABA levels, which is a neurotransmitter that helps to regulate calmness and relaxation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures that help with anxiety, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including Camellia sinensis leaves (also known as green tea leaves), a carrier oil like coconut or grapeseed oil, a glass jar with a lid, and a dark glass bottle for storage. Begin by drying the Camellia sinensis leaves in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for a few hours or by air-drying them for several days. Once the leaves are dry, chop them finely and place them in the glass jar. Pour the carrier oil over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible. Discard the solids and your Camellia sinensis tincture is ready to use. You can take 1-2 teaspoons of the tincture in water or tea to help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for anxiety

Kaempferol has been shown to be beneficial in reducing anxiety due to its ability to interact with brain receptors that regulate mood, resulting in a decrease in stress hormones and an increase in the production of neurotransmitters that promote relaxation.

Tannins are good for anxiety because they have been shown to have a calming effect on the body by reducing stress hormones and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and promote a sense of well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help people with anxiety. The tincture made from this plant contains chemicals that can affect the brain's chemistry and help calm the mind. It is thought to work by increasing the levels of serotonin in the brain, a chemical that helps regulate mood. When serotonin levels are low, people may feel anxious, depressed, and irritable. By increasing serotonin, Hypericum perforatum tincture can help reduce feelings of anxiety and promote a sense of calm. Additionally, it has been shown to have an anti-inflammatory effect, which can also contribute to its anxiety-reducing properties. It's also worth noting that many people find it helpful when taken in conjunction with other natural remedies, such as passionflower or valerian root.  Note: Before taking any herbal remedy, especially one that can interact with prescription medications, it's best to consult a healthcare professional to discuss the potential benefits and risks.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures that help with anxiety, start by gathering dried Camellia sinensis leaves, also known as green tea leaves. You will also need a glass jar, a dark place to store it, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Begin by measuring one part of dried Camellia sinensis leaves to five parts of the solvent, and then place the leaves in the jar. Pour the solvent over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered, and then seal the jar tightly. Store the jar in a dark place for at least two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the leaves release their medicinal properties into the solvent. After the two to three weeks are up, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. You now have a Camellia sinensis tincture that can be taken sublingually - by placing a few drops under your tongue - to help calm anxiety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with anxiety. Ginger tincture can be taken before bed to help with sleep, or during the day to help manage anxiety symptoms. It's also a natural and non-habit-forming alternative to traditional anxiety medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tincture, also known as hops, is a natural remedy that can help with anxiety. It contains a group of chemicals called flavonoids and bitter acids that have a calming effect on the body. When taken in small amounts, hops tincture can slow down the heart rate and lower blood pressure, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress. It also has a sedative effect on the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep, which is often a problem for people with anxiety. Additionally, hops tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body, a known contributor to anxiety. Hops tincture is often used in combination with other herbal remedies, such as valerian root and passionflower, to create a calming and soothing effect on the body. The tincture can be taken in small amounts, usually 20-30 drops, and can be added to water or taken sublingually for immediate effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tinctures that help with anxiety, start by gathering dried Camellia sinensis leaves and a high-proof spirit like vodka or rum. Combine 1 part of the dried leaves with 2 parts of the spirit in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered by the liquid. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place, shaking it well every day for 2-4 weeks to allow the herbs to infuse into the spirit. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be stored in the fridge to slow down the aging process, and is typically taken in small amounts, usually 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, under the tongue or mixed with water, to help alleviate anxiety symptoms.

The tincture form of this plant is made by extracting its active compounds and turning them into a concentrated liquid. When taken, the tincture can promote relaxation and calmness by interacting with the brain's neurotransmitters. It is believed to increase the levels of serotonin and dopamine, which are chemicals that help regulate mood and reduce stress. As a result, people who take Camellia sinensis tincture may feel more centered and grounded, and their anxiety may decrease. The tincture can also have a sedative effect, helping to quiet the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. This can be particularly helpful for people who experience anxiety-related insomnia. Camellia sinensis tincture is often used in herbal medicine and aromatherapy to promote emotional balance and well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tincture for anxiety, start by gathering high-quality tea leaves from the Camellia sinensis plant, also known as green tea. You will also need a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid, a carrier oil like glycerin or vodka, and a dark glass bottle for storing the tincture. Begin by drying the tea leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Once the leaves are dry, place them in the glass jar, covering them with the carrier oil. Make sure the leaves are completely submerged in the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful way to manage anxiety. One herb that's often used is Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root. This herb has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress. It works by increasing the levels of a chemical called GABA in the brain, which helps to slow down thoughts and promote relaxation.  Another herb used to help with anxiety is Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower. This herb has a similar effect to valerian root, and can help to reduce anxiety and promote a good night's sleep. Passionflower works by interacting with neurotransmitters in the brain, which can help to calm the mind and body.  Fixing anxiety issues is important because it can impact many areas of life. When you're feeling anxious, it can be hard to focus on work or school, and you may struggle to form relationships or enjoy activities. This can lead to feelings of isolation and low self-esteem. By managing anxiety with herbal tinctures, you can improve your ability to focus and concentrate, and feel more confident and capable.  Using herbal tinctures can be a good way to start managing anxiety, but it's also important to talk to a healthcare professional about your symptoms. They can help you determine the best course of treatment and provide support and guidance as you work to manage your anxiety.
supplementary_best: For anxiety, a combination of Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, and Lavandula angustifolia tinctures may be effective. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that helps reduce anxiety and promotes a good night's sleep. Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, is a calming herb that can soothe the nervous system and reduce anxiety symptoms. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.  This combination is chosen because it addresses anxiety from multiple angles. Valeriana officinalis helps with physical relaxation, while Passiflora incarnata and Lavandula angustifolia help with mental relaxation and calmness. The combination of these three herbs can provide a balanced and effective approach to managing anxiety.
supplementary_avoid: If you have anxiety, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. Kava (Piper methysticum) tincture, which was mentioned earlier, is not on the excluded list, but we are excluding it here, so let's move on to other tinctures.  Tinctures made from Yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe) bark can be problematic for people with anxiety. This is because yohimbe can cause increased heart rate, blood pressure, and other side effects that might worsen anxiety symptoms. Yohimbe tincture can interact with certain medications, making anxiety worse.  Another tincture to avoid is Ephedra (Ephedra sinica) tincture. Ephedra has been linked to increased anxiety, panic attacks, and other negative effects in some individuals. It's essential to be cautious when using ephedra products, especially if you have anxiety or other health conditions.  Tinctures made from Ephedra are often combined with other herbs like Ma Huang (Ephedra sinica) or with ephedrine, a stimulant that can worsen anxiety. Even though the ephedrine has been banned in some countries, it is still present in some herbal products and can exacerbate anxiety symptoms.  Lastly, tinctures made from Ginseng (Panax spp.) should be used with caution if you have anxiety. While some people find ginseng helpful for stress and fatigue, it can also cause increased anxiety in others, especially if taken in high doses. If you have anxiety, consult with a healthcare professional before using ginseng tinctures.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aromatherapy with essential oils like lavender and chamomile can help reduce anxiety. These oils are absorbed through the skin, which affects the brain's emotional centers. When inhaled, they calm the nervous system, slowing down heart rate and blood pressure. This helps alleviate anxiety symptoms like rapid heartbeat and trembling.  GABA supplements, or gamma-aminobutyric acid, are another natural remedy. GABA is a neurotransmitter that helps calm the brain, reducing anxiety and stress. Taking GABA supplements can increase its levels in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety symptoms. Magnesium supplements are also helpful, as they relax muscles and calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress.  Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that helps the body adapt to stress. It increases the production of neurotransmitters that calm the brain, reducing anxiety and stress. Ashwagandha also helps regulate sleep patterns, which is often affected by anxiety.  Exercise, especially yoga, can also help reduce anxiety. Physical activity releases endorphins, which are natural mood-boosters. Yoga combines physical exercise with deep breathing and meditation techniques, which calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety symptoms.  Bath salts and Epsom salts contain magnesium, which helps relax muscles and calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress. Taking a warm bath with these salts can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms.  A healthy diet that includes omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin B, and complex carbohydrates can also help reduce anxiety. These nutrients promote healthy brain function and reduce inflammation, which is associated with anxiety.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with anxiety often experience other health problems. They may have insomnia, or trouble sleeping. This can be helped by the herb valerian root, which has a calming effect on the body. Valerian root also helps to slow down brain activity, making it easier to fall asleep.  Some people with anxiety may experience headaches or migraines. Feverfew is a medicinal herb that can help with these symptoms. It reduces the amount of a chemical called serotonin in the blood, which can cause headaches. Feverfew also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain.  Anxiety can also cause stomach problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Peppermint oil can help with IBS symptoms. It relaxes the muscles in the stomach and helps to ease cramps and bloating.  Anxiety can lead to depression, which is a low mood that lasts for a long time. St. John's Wort is a medicinal herb that can help with depression symptoms. It increases the amount of serotonin in the blood, which helps to improve mood.  Some people with anxiety may experience muscle tension or muscle pain. Ginger can help with these symptoms. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation. Ginger also relaxes the muscles and helps to ease tension.  People with anxiety may also experience an increased heart rate. Ashwagandha is a medicinal herb that can help with this symptom. It reduces stress and anxiety by increasing the amount of a chemical called GABA in the brain. GABA helps to calm the body and slow down the heart rate.  Ashwagandha also helps to lower blood pressure, which can be a problem for people with anxiety. It does this by reducing the amount of stress hormones in the body, such as cortisol. When cortisol levels are high, blood pressure can rise.  Kava is another medicinal herb that can help with anxiety symptoms. It has a calming effect on the body and can help to reduce anxiety and stress. Kava also helps to lower blood pressure and can improve sleep quality.  Note: Before using any medicinal herb, consult a healthcare professional for guidance.
